items.title: Why are the world's biggest AI firms so attracted to the NT?

children.content: The NT's sunny skies, natural gas reserves and proximity to Asia have caught the attention of a dozen data centre developers.

articles.title: We're back to two competing superpowers and China is pulling ahead

children.content: When it comes to AI, Australia is a spectator at a UFC cage fight between the United States and China, except the spectators are inside the cage with the combatants.

image.altText: The US flag is waving in front of the red and gold round symbol of China

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

articles.title: 'Hyperscale' data centre planned for remote NT

children.content: The proposed 19,150-hectare 'Project Ares' would be built by Energy North on Murranji Station in the Barkly Region, as up to 12 proponents eye the NT for data centres.

image.altText: a gate with a cut-out of a man on a horse silhouetted against a sunset.

gallery.altText: A computer-generated image of a huge industrial site in an outback area.

thumbnail.altText: A computer-generated image of a huge industrial site in an outback area.

avatar.alt: A man with light-coloured eyes and a moustache looks down the barrel of the camera.

items.title: Man dies on APY Lands after large fight breaks out, police say

children.content: A man has died after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, with police investigating whether his death was the result of a medical episode.

image.altText: A drone of houses with mountain range behind them.

items.title: NT government facing Federal Court over 'draconian' compensation cap laws

children.content: The NT government's controversial laws capping compensation for prisoners unlawfully assaulted, strip searched or imprisoned are being challenged in the Federal Court.

image.altText: A building painted in black and brown reads: NAAJA.

items.title: Shock resignation of NT children's commissioner deemed 'a tragedy'

children.content: Australia's peak advocate for Aboriginal children has described the circumstances around child protection in the Northern Territory as "dire", following the shock resignation of the Territory's children's commissioner.

image.altText: A woman, looking serious, leaning against a white wall covered in children's painted handprints in bright colours.

items.title: Crocs, sharks and an Aussie cricket legend: All the action from the Barra Classic

children.content: Crocs, sharks and an Australian cricket legend have all turned up for one of Australia's biggest barra fishing tournaments on the Daly River.

image.altText: Three fishers, two standing, one sitting on a boat, floating along green water.

items.title: Police release video of killer Bradley Murdoch filmed weeks before his death

children.content: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of Peter Falconio's remains.

image.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

gallery.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

thumbnail.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

items.title: As swift parrots kept breeding in this forest, the government realised it had a problem

children.content: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

gallery.altText: A fledgling swift parrot pokes its head out of a tree hollow.

thumbnail.altText: A fledgling swift parrot pokes its head out of a tree hollow.

items.title: Sinner triumphs over Zverev for second consecutive Wimbledon win

children.content: The top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Alexander Zverev for his second consecutive title at Wimbledon after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue.

image.altText: Jannik Sinner with his hands over his head after he won at Wimbledon.

items.title: Change to international travel as digital arrival cards roll out

children.content: A digital alternative to incoming passenger cards filled out by everyone entering Australia is to be implemented in the next two years.

image.altText: People walk under the arrivals sign at Hobart Airport

items.title: Albanese has the Coalition exactly where he wants it

children.content: When the bells resume ringing in the halls of parliament, Anthony Albanese and Angus Taylor will both do well to dispose of any complacency.

image.altText: A close up of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a white hard hat and a yellow high-vis vest.

items.title: Jewish academic fears being met by 'axe or gun' when on campus

children.content: Jewish academics and students say they do not feel safe on campuses, as the antisemitism royal commission prepares to hold a week of hearings scrutinising universities.

image.altText: The view through an archway and onto a university courtyard

items.title: Bellingham goes big time again as England continues down hard road

children.content: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

articles.title: Why Norway feels 'bitter' after contest of controversy against England

children.content: Norway felt England's equaliser shouldn't have stood after the ball appeared to get a deflection off a wire holding up the "spidercam" above the pitch, directly leading to England gaining possession and scoring.

image.altText: Norway players surround the referee

articles.title: Swiss coach blasts 'unacceptable' VAR rule after World Cup elimination

children.content: Switzerland is fuming after Breel Embolo was sent of the highly charged World Cup quarterfinal against Argentina in Kansas City.

image.altText: Referee Joao Pinheiro shows a red card to Breel Embolo of Switzerland at the World Cup.

articles.title: Quick hits: A tearful red, a perfect 10 and a massive cast

children.content: Norway fumes at England's equaliser, Breel Embolo's tearful red card and Jordan Henderson's heavy-duty cast. Here are today's quick hits from the World Cup.

items.title: Government to crack down on antisemitism on university campuses

children.content: The new standards will require institutions to adopt definitions of antisemitism, racism against Aboriginal and Torres Straight Islander peoples, and Islamophobia.

items.title: Businesses caught between Labor's trust tax and stamp duty

children.content: Small businesses affected by the change are in limbo as states decide whether to waive stamp duty fees, with industry groups warning relief will be limited unless they sign on.

items.title: Bangkok pub fire kills at least 27 people and injures 63

children.content: A huge fire engulfed a pub in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people before firefighters brought the blaze under control.

image.altText: Rescuers stand outside a closed pub with a bright, colourful facade and the words 'Blend 285' on the front.

items.title: How setting small, specific goals can help reduce your drinking

children.content: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

children.content: From elephant tracking in Botswana to running up mountains in Nepal, catch up on every week six film from Race Around the World.

items.title: Camp to boost female firefighter participation ditched after one year

children.content: The free camp ran over a weekend in June 2024 for students in years 9 and 10, exploring firefighting activities, road crashes, swiftwater rescues, and physical fitness training.

items.title: Lindsey Graham, pivotal US Republican senator, dies suddenly at 71

children.content: US senator Lindsey Graham, a key Republican who went from a vocal critic of Donald Trump to one of his most loyal allies, has died at age 71.

image.altText: Men in suits and ties, the one in the centre smiles at another.

items.title: Melbourne cafe catches fire three days after alleged drive-by shooting

children.content: Police are treating a fire at an Essendon North cafe as suspicious, just days after the business was sprayed with bullets.

image.altText: A firefighter at a cafe

items.title: Family of Perth man raises questions over his Bali death

children.content: The family of Perth man Cameron Hughes, who died in a Bali immigration detention centre, says it has many questions about the circumstances surrounding his death.

image.altText: A man in a pale blue shirt sitting at a venue holding a menu

items.title: Convicted paedophile to argue multiple-personality evidence unlawful

children.content: A father jailed for abusing two children is appealing against his conviction, arguing evidence given by the multiple personalities of the same witness was unlawful.

image.altText: A court sketch of a man with his face blurred.

items.title: Mate recalls Richard Scolyer's 'magnificent' ride ahead of memorial service

children.content: As Mark Beretta rode with all his might up a steep Tasmanian mountain in March, it was none other than Richard Scolyer who overtook him.

gallery.altText: Mark Beretta and Richard Scolyer in black lyra, smiling as they stand near a paddock in Tasmania.

thumbnail.altText: Mark Beretta and Richard Scolyer in black lyra, smiling as they stand near a paddock in Tasmania.

items.title: 'Like my men's shed': John never left the soccer club he helped create

children.content: For more than 30 years, John O'Toole has quietly helped build a local soccer club. On Sunday, he surpassed his 600th match while playing alongside his sons.

image.altText: A man with white hair and a long white beard wearing orange soccer colours on a soccer field.

items.title: Coalition promises to focus on repairing Victorian roads if elected

children.content: The Liberals and Nationals are promising to make Victoria's roads a top priority if elected in November.

image.altText: A road with potholes filled with water.

items.title: Illegal camping fine of $345 divides coastal community

children.content: A crackdown on illegal camping in Noosa, prompted by hundreds of complaints, has sparked outrage locally, with many arguing fines will not solve the issue.

items.title: SA doctor banned for inappropriate relationship with client

children.content: A former Adelaide doctor will not be able to reapply for registration for at least two years after he was banned for having an inappropriate relationship with his client.

items.title: Expanding Sydney's tree canopy could save $1b a year in health costs

children.content: A series of greenways crisscrossing Sydney's west presents an opportunity to grow the city's canopy and make more public space, a Committee for Sydney report suggests.

items.title: UK police arrest 28-year-old man in Ann Widdecombe murder case

items.title: Theoretical 'mirror life' could grow out of control, scientists fear

children.content: This year, scientists included a new potential threat in their decision to move the Doomsday Clock closer to midnight. So what is 'mirror life' and why are they concerned?
